records.ety: [L. subjugatus, p. p. of subjugare to subjugate; sub under + jugum a yoke. See Yoke.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Subjugated (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Subjugating.]

defs: To subdue, and bring under the yoke of power or dominion; to conquer by force, and compel to submit to the government or absolute control of another; to vanquish.
quotes: He subjugated a king, and called him his “vassal.”
